response: We propose a new method for [task], aiming to improve performance over strong existing baselines. Evaluated on the [dataset] test set across five random seeds, our approach achieves an average accuracy of 73.4 ± 1.1, compared to 72.2 ± 0.9 for a strong baseline, corresponding to a +1.2 absolute improvement. However, a two-sample t-test indicates that this difference is not statistically significant (t = 1.04, p = 0.31; 95% CI of the gain: [–1.1, +3.5]). These results suggest that while our method offers a modest numerical improvement, the evidence does not rule out the possibility that the observed gain is due to chance. We release our code to support further investigation and reproducibility. Our findings highlight the importance of rigorous statistical evaluation when assessing incremental improvements in [task] performance.
